_invariant: Real Morpho-style exposure graph for stETH (Lido LST) at the 2022-06-13 Celsius/3AC deleveraging depeg. stETH supply REAL on-chain (4,219,980.057 @ block 14955879, 2022-06-13T11:59:57Z, 18dp, Alchemy archive). One isolated lending collateral edge with a MARKET-PRICE stETH/ETH oracle (depeg_sensitive=true), the regime where looped stETH/ETH positions face liquidation as the ratio falls. stETH is pegged to ETH not USD: the per-case peg_probe price is the stETH/ETH ratio (peg=1.0 = 1 ETH-equiv), MEASURED on-chain (Curve stETH/ETH get_dy) at each case's own anchor block. by_design_discount is FALSE: this was a market liquidity dislocation under forced deleveraging, NOT a contractual discount and NOT a backing failure (every stETH still 1:1 staked-ETH backed). RE-ANCHORED 2026-06: the snapshot block moved from 14955378 (10:00Z, where the Curve venue had partially recovered to 0.9434, ~5.7% — short of the documented trough) to 14955879 (12:00Z), the on-chain depth-trough block where the marginal stETH/ETH = 0.93501 (~6.5% below peg) matches the documented forensic trough.

meta.data_source: on-chain Alchemy archive (stETH 0xae7ab9...fe84 @ block 14955879, totalSupply 4,219,980.057 stETH, 18dp) + an isolated stETH/ETH lending market modeled on the Aave v2 stETH collateral regime (market-price oracle, looped positions). Depeg ratio per-case MEASURED on-chain (Curve stETH/ETH pool get_dy) at the case's own anchor block.

metadata._note: Liquid staking token pegged to ETH (1 stETH = 1 staked-ETH-equiv). In June 2022 NO native withdrawals (enabled only at Shanghai, Apr 2023) -> only exit was the secondary market (Curve stETH/ETH + CEX). Terra->Celsius->3AC forced deleveraging dumped stETH into thin liquidity; the ETH-relative ratio fell to ~0.93-0.935 (on-chain trough Mon Jun 13 ~12:00-15:00Z). No backing failure: every stETH still 1:1 staked-ETH backed. totalSupply 4,219,980.057 @ block 14955879 (2022-06-13T11:59:57Z, Alchemy archive).

Models the Aave-v2-style stETH/ETH leveraged-loop collateral regime: a market-price stETH/ETH oracle, so the secondary-market discount flows into LTV and pressures looped positions during the cascade.

oracle.oracle_provider: Market-price stETH/ETH (secondary-market ratio) — tracks the real Curve/CEX discount, NOT a hardcoded 1.0 exchange rate

meta.data_source: stETH supply on-chain @ 14955879 (4,219,980.057); isolated stETH/ETH lending market (market-price oracle, LT 0.90) modeling the loop-collateral regime exposed to the secondary-market discount.
